text.title: ops-customer-success-metrics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-customer-success-metrics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Computing health score on a per-CSM basis without a canonical formula, leading to score inflation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inconsistent scoring breaks early warning systems. Formulaic scoring enables cross-team calibration and prevents subjective grade inflation.

failure-mode.description: Running continuous always-on NPS surveys, polling every customer weekly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Continuous NPS creates survey fatigue; response rate collapses after 2-3 weeks. Quarterly cadence is the sweet spot: frequent enough to catch trends, sparse enough that customers don't tune out.

failure-mode.description: Only responding to customers who reach out via support; ignoring usage drops and silent warning signs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reactive CS misses 60% of at-risk accounts that decline silently without contacting support. Proactive monitoring surfaces issues 30-60 days before churn, when interventions are still possible.

failure-mode.description: Treating expansion as a product or sales function; CS team is measured only on retention/churn, not revenue growth.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CS teams closest to customers have the best visibility into expansion needs. When CS is excluded from expansion revenue tracking, they optimize for retention alone and miss revenue opportunities.
